function.name: getUserAccounts
function.description: Fetch bank accounts for a user with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of accounts per page (default: 20, max: 100)

function.name: getUserAccountData
function.description: Fetch account data for a user

function.name: getUserTransactions
function.description: Retrieve transactions for a user account in a date window with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of transactions per page (default: 50, max: 200)

function.name: getRecurringForecasts
function.description: Get recurring/forecast transactions for an account with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of forecasts per page (default: 25, max: 100)

function.name: getUpcomingTransactions
function.description: Get upcoming transactions for an account over a date range with pagination support to prevent memory issues.

limit.description: Number of transactions per page (default: 30, max: 100)

function.name: getTransactionSummary
function.description: Get transaction summary statistics without loading full transaction data to prevent memory issues.

function.name: getSelectedKeacastAccounts
function.description: Get the selected Keacast accounts for a user.

function.name: createTransaction
function.description: Create a forecasted income or expense on the user's currently selected account. Estimation happens at PROPOSE time only: estimate any value you weren't given (title, category, date, frequency) from the conversation, account context, and the user's similar/recurring/recent transactions — but you MUST always supply a concrete non-zero amount and an explicit type. CRITICAL: this tool writes real data, so do NOT call it until you have shown the user the full proposed transaction (title, type, amount, start date, frequency if recurring) and they have EXPLICITLY confirmed (call confirmTransaction first when they do). On the confirmation turn, copy the EXACT values from your proposal message — same amount, same date, same category, same title; never re-estimate them (a mismatched amount is refused). On the turn the user first asks, DO NOT call this — propose the estimated details and ask them to confirm or adjust; only call it after they say yes. Pass amount as a positive number and set type ('expense' or 'income'); the system applies the correct sign. Recurrence is controlled ONLY by frequency (e.g. 'every month' => 28-31, 'weekly' => 7); omitted frequency means one-time. Omitted fields default: start=today, end=start for one-time or start+1 year for recurring, category/title/description from context, time=12:00. Always warn about any resulting negative forecasted balances.

title.description: Short name, e.g. 'Rent' or 'Paycheck'. Estimate from context if not stated.

type.description: expense for money spent, income for money received. Estimate from context if not stated.

amount.description: Positive magnitude of the transaction; the system signs it based on type. Estimate from similar/recurring transactions if the user didn't give one.

description.description: Optional details. Defaults to the title.

start.description: Date it occurs/starts (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO). Defaults to today.

end.description: Recurring only: when recurrence stops (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO). Defaults to start+1 year.

time.description: Time of day HH:mm. Defaults to 12:00.

function.name: proposeSimulationAdd
function.description: SIMULATION (what-if) tool — stage a HYPOTHETICAL new income or expense in the user's simulation overlay. This does NOT write any data; the user sees the change on their calendar with projected balances and commits or discards it themselves. Use this instead of createTransaction whenever the user is in Simulation Mode or asks a hypothetical/what-if question (e.g. 'what if I added a $500/month savings transfer?'). No confirmation turn is needed — propose immediately with your best estimates for any missing fields, exactly like createTransaction's estimation rules (amount as a positive number, type expense/income, frequency codes, start defaults to today).

title.description: Short name, e.g. 'Rent' or 'Paycheck'. Estimate from context if not stated.

type.description: expense for money spent, income for money received.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; the system signs it based on type. Estimate if not stated.

category.description: Best-fit category from the user's available categories.

description.description: Optional details. Defaults to the title.

start.description: Date it occurs/starts (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO). Defaults to today.

end.description: Recurring only: when recurrence stops. Defaults to start+1 year.

time.description: Time of day HH:mm. Defaults to 12:00.

function.name: proposeSimulationModify
function.description: SIMULATION (what-if) tool — stage a HYPOTHETICAL change to an EXISTING forecasted transaction (by transactionid) in the user's simulation overlay. Does NOT write any data and needs NO confirmation turn — the user reviews the change on their calendar and commits or discards it. Use instead of updateTransaction whenever the user is in Simulation Mode or asks a what-if about changing an existing forecast (e.g. 'what if my rent went up to $2000?'). Set scope to 'group' to change every occurrence of a recurring forecast, 'groupfrom' to change this and future occurrences, or 'single' (default) for just this one. Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type F/RF) can be simulated.

transactionid.description: The id of the existing forecasted transaction to change.

groupid.description: The recurring group id, when changing a recurring forecast's group.

scope.description: single=this occurrence only (default), group=every occurrence, groupfrom=this and future occurrences.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; system applies sign from type.

end.description: Recurring only: new end of the recurrence.

function.name: proposeSimulationRemove
function.description: SIMULATION (what-if) tool — stage a HYPOTHETICAL removal of an EXISTING forecasted transaction (by transactionid) in the user's simulation overlay. Does NOT delete any data and needs NO confirmation turn — the user reviews the removal on their calendar and commits or discards it. Use instead of deleteTransaction whenever the user is in Simulation Mode or asks a what-if about dropping a forecast (e.g. 'what if I cancelled my gym membership?'). Set scope to 'group' to remove every occurrence of a recurring forecast, 'groupfrom' to remove this and future occurrences, or 'single' (default) for just this one. Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type F/RF) can be simulated.

transactionid.description: The id of the existing forecasted transaction to remove.

groupid.description: The recurring group id, when removing a recurring forecast's group.

scope.description: single=this occurrence only (default), group=every occurrence, groupfrom=this and future occurrences.

function.name: confirmTransaction
function.description: NON-WRITING signal that the user's LATEST message confirms the transaction you proposed on a PRIOR turn (e.g. 'yes', 'go ahead', 'add it', 'looks good', 'yes but make it $45'). Call this FIRST on the confirmation turn, then immediately call createTransaction (or updateTransaction) with the confirmed draft values. If the user confirmed but also adjusted a value ('yes, make it $45 instead'), call updateDraftTransaction with the adjusted value BEFORE calling this. Do NOT call this when the user is still deciding, asking a question, or describing a brand-new transaction.

note.description: Optional: one short phrase quoting what the user said that constitutes the confirmation.

function.name: updateDraftTransaction
function.description: NON-WRITING scratchpad for slot-filling. Record or refine the fields of a transaction the user is PLANNING as details emerge across the conversation (a planned purchase, an estimated amount, a target month). Only record values that came from THIS conversation. This does NOT create or change any real data — it only updates the assistant's working draft so the user never has to repeat themselves. Call it whenever a new detail appears. Set pendingConfirmation=true ONLY when you have just proposed a complete, concrete transaction (single amount) and are asking the user to confirm on their next message.

intent.description: Short label for what the user is planning, e.g. 'weekly gas budget'.

type.description: expense for money spent, income for money received.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; a SINGLE concrete estimate, never a range.

start.description: YYYY-MM-DD or ISO date it occurs/starts.

transactionid.description: Only when staging an edit to an EXISTING transaction (for updateTransaction).

pendingConfirmation.description: Set true ONLY when you have proposed a complete concrete transaction and are awaiting the user's yes/no.

function.name: getGoals
function.description: Fetch the user's savings goals on the selected account, with derived progress: target_amount, accumulated_amount, remaining_amount, progress_pct, days_remaining, next_contribution, expected_by_now, and on_track (whether accumulated savings match what the schedule expects by today). Use this whenever the user asks about a goal, their savings progress, or when giving financial-planning advice that should account for money already earmarked toward goals. Report the derived numbers as-is — do not recompute them.

function.name: previewGoalCadence
function.description: NON-WRITING savings-plan calculator. Given a target amount, a date window, and a contribution frequency, returns exactly how much the user must set aside per contribution and on which dates (occurrences, amount_per_row, dates). Use it to answer questions like 'how much per paycheck to save $3000 by December?' with real math instead of estimating, and to preview a plan BEFORE proposing createGoal. Always sanity-check the per-contribution amount against the user's upcoming forecasted balances and warn if it would cause a negative balance.

target_amount.description: Total amount the user wants to save (positive).

start_date.description: First contribution date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

frequency.description: Contribution cadence: '1' daily, '7' weekly, '14' bi-weekly, '15' semi-monthly (1st & 15th), '28'-'31' monthly anchored to start_date's day, '60' bi-monthly, '91' quarterly, '182' semi-annual, '365' annual.

function.name: createGoal
function.description: Create a REAL savings goal on the user's selected account. The system schedules the contributions between start_date and end_date at the given frequency, and they appear on the user's calendar as forecasted set-asides. This writes real data: like createTransaction, you MUST first propose the full goal (title, target amount, deadline, cadence and the per-contribution amount from previewGoalCadence) and get the user's explicit confirmation on a later turn (call confirmTransaction first when they confirm). Goals are savings-style only. Before proposing, verify the per-contribution amount doesn't push the user's forecasted balances negative — warn them if it would.

title.description: Short goal name, e.g. 'Vacation fund' or 'Emergency fund'.

category.description: Category label for the goal. Defaults to 'Savings'.

description.description: Optional details about what the goal is for.

start_date.description: First contribution date (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

function.name: updateGoal
function.description: Update an EXISTING savings goal by goalid (change its target, deadline, cadence, or name). Writes real data — propose the change and get the user's explicit confirmation on a later turn first (call confirmTransaction when they confirm). Changing target/frequency/dates automatically redistributes the remaining unlocked future contributions. Find the goalid via getGoals.

goalid.description: The id of the goal to update.

function.name: deleteGoal
function.description: Delete an EXISTING savings goal by goalid (removes its scheduled future contributions from the calendar). Destructive — always confirm with the user on a later turn before calling (call confirmTransaction when they confirm). Find the goalid via getGoals.

goalid.description: The id of the goal to delete.

function.name: rememberFact
function.description: Persist a DURABLE fact about the user for future conversations (a savings goal, a planned project and its estimated cost, income cadence, risk tolerance, a stated preference). Only save what the user actually stated or clearly implied — never guesses, and never transient chit-chat. Prefer a short stable mem_key so re-saving updates the same fact.

mem_value.description: Concise fact text worth remembering long-term.

importance.description: 1-10; higher means more important to retain.

accountScoped.description: true if the fact is specific to the currently selected account; omit/false for user-level facts.

function.name: recallFacts
function.description: Retrieve durable facts previously saved about the user (most important/recent first). Use only when you need more of the user's saved context than is already shown in the LONG-TERM MEMORY block.

function.name: updateTransaction
function.description: Update an EXISTING forecasted transaction by transactionid (e.g. change its amount, date, title, category, or frequency). Writes real data, so you MUST first propose the change and get the user's explicit confirmation on a LATER turn before calling this (enforced in code). Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type 'F'/'RF') can be updated. Pass amount as a positive number and set type; the system applies the sign.

transactionid.description: The id of the transaction to update.

amount.description: Positive magnitude; system applies sign from type.

function.name: suggestShoppingItemOptions
function.description: NON-WRITING Smart Price Assist for shopping-list items. Given an item name (e.g. 'Milk'), returns 3-5 realistic purchase options across major retailers (store, brand, product, size, estimatedPrice, confidence), a normalized item name, a suggested tax category/taxability, and a flag when the user's own estimate looks unrealistic. Prices are ESTIMATES, never live prices, and are NEVER applied automatically — the user chooses an option or keeps their manual estimate. Use when the user asks what an item costs or wants price suggestions for a shopping list.

itemName.description: The shopping item as the user typed it, e.g. 'milk' or 'paper towels'.

quantity.description: How many the user plans to buy (default 1).

region.description: Shopper location for regional pricing, when known.

userEstimate.description: The user's current unit-price estimate, to sanity-check for unrealistic values.

function.name: openTransactionSearch
function.description: NON-WRITING UI action: opens the Keacast transaction search panel on the user's screen, optionally pre-filled with a search term that runs immediately. Use when the user asks you to open search or to find/look up/show transactions IN THE APP (e.g. 'search for my Uber transactions', 'open search', 'pull up my Netflix charges'). The chat window minimizes and the search results appear front and center. This does NOT return transaction data to you — when YOU need the data to answer a question, use the read tools (getUserTransactions, getUpcomingTransactions, getRecurringForecasts) instead. After calling this, tell the user briefly that the search is opening; do not invent result counts or amounts.

search_term.description: Text to pre-fill and run in the search box (merchant name, transaction title, category, amount, etc.). Omit to just open the empty search panel.

function.name: deleteTransaction
function.description: Deletes a FORECASTED transaction (or a whole recurring series) from the user's account. This writes real data and is confirm-gated in code: first FIND the transaction (check the RECENT WRITES THIS SESSION block for ids of transactions you just created, else look it up via getUpcomingTransactions with a date window around the date the user mentioned, or getRecurringForecasts), show the user exactly what you found (title, amount, date), and — if it is recurring — ask whether to delete just that occurrence or the entire series. Only after the user explicitly confirms (call confirmTransaction) call this tool. Use scope 'single' with transactionid for one occurrence; scope 'group' with groupid to delete every occurrence of the series. Never claim a transaction doesn't exist until a lookup with a correct date window returned no match. Only forecasted transactions (forecast_type 'F') can be deleted — actual transactions ('A') cannot.

transactionid.description: The id of the forecasted transaction occurrence to delete (required for scope 'single').

scope.description: 'single' (default) deletes one occurrence by transactionid; 'group' deletes the ENTIRE recurring series by groupid.

groupid.description: The recurring series' group id (required for scope 'group'). Found on transactions returned by the read tools and in RECENT WRITES.
